titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 571

p.seg: David, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all Holy Church's faithful, present and to come, greeting.

Know me to have given and conceded to the church of St Mary and St Cuthbert at Coldingham, and to the monastic community serving there, Edrom and Nisbet in perpetual alms, just as Cospatric the brother of Dolphin held them on the day he was alive and dead, with their correct boundaries, as free and quit as they hold other lands which belong to Coldingham, in churches, waters, and meadows.

Present as witnesses: Herbert the abbot of Roxburgh, Daniel the prior of Jedburgh, Ascelin the archdeacon of Glasgow, William the son of Duncan, Duncan the earl, Hugh de Morville, Gervase Ridel, Alan son of Waldeve, and Cospatric his own brother, William de Somerville, William of Graham.
At Roxburgh on the 17th day before the kalends of September [16th August] in the year 1139.
